message:
test: add 11 transcript component tests and close component test epic (bd-1p9c, bd-1kl7)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

All modules are pure logic — search engine (BM25, tokenizer, hash-embedder), export (JSON, YAML, markdown, skills), templates, prompts registry, cost calculation. These can ALL be tested with zero mocks. 5 subtasks covering all modules.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:57Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1o83","title":"refactor: hook tests to use shared realistic test fixtures instead of per-test vi.fn() fetch mocks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:28:49.679329978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:27.569031193Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o83","depends_on_id":"bd-tr6k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:30:23.156949151Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":74,"issue_id":"bd-1o83","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SCOPE: Refactor hook tests (useRating, useReviews, useLeaderboard) to use shared realistic fixtures instead of per-test vi.fn() fetch mocks. APPROACH: Create a test-utils/fetch-fixtures.ts with realistic API response shapes. Reduce mock boilerplate — hooks should be tested against consistent, realistic data shapes, not ad-hoc fakes per test. BLOCKED BY bd-tr6k — need hook testing patterns established first.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:27Z"}]}

APPROACH: Provide real transcript data fixtures, render components, verify structure. Complex but testable — test with realistic session data.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:31:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ppu","title":"Backend: Budget persistence + hard stop enforcement (Premium)","description":"Implement premium backend storage + enforcement for budget settings. Scope: API endpoints to read/write budget caps + alerts, persist per-user settings, enforce hard-stop on overages for prompt runs, and expose audit log for alerts. Mirror bead in premium repo and link cross-repo dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:43.856815347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:05:31.256945535Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:05:31.256921389Z","close_reason":"Spec complete in this repo; backend persistence/enforcement must be implemented in premium repo. Public repo follow-ups (CLI/web consuming /cli/budgets when available) should be tracked separately once endpoints land.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Draft backend spec for budgets/hard stop (premium repo):

Data model:
- budgets: user_id (pk), monthly_cap_usd, per_run_cap_usd, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled, created_at, updated_at.
- budget_alerts: id, user_id, alert_type (per_run|monthly|hard_stop), cap_usd, total_cost_usd, currency, prompt_id, prompt_title, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, source (estimate|actual), created_at.
- usage_rollups: user_id, month (YYYY-MM), total_cost_usd, total_tokens, last_run_at.

API (premium):
- GET /cli/budgets -> { budgets, usage_month, usage, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled }.
- PUT /cli/budgets -> update caps/toggles; return saved payload.
- GET /cli/budget-alerts?limit=&before= -> list alerts for UI/CLI.
- POST /cli/budget-alerts -> optional client-side logging for cost estimates (optional).

Enforcement:
- On any prompt run/execute endpoint, compute projected cost. If hard_stop_enabled and projected monthly total > cap -> return 402/403 with code budget_exceeded and include caps + current usage.
- If per_run cap exceeded, allow run but record alert (and return warning in response).
- Monthly usage should be computed from actual usage only (estimates should not increment usage).

Client integration notes (public repo):
- CLI currently logs alerts locally to ~/.config/jfp/budget-alerts.jsonl.
- Once backend exists, update CLI status to pull budgets/usage from /cli/budgets for premium users; local config remains fallback.
- Web budget settings page is local-only; wire to backend when endpoints exist.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:03Z"},{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional spec details:

Response shapes:
- GET /cli/budgets -> { ok: true, data: { budgets: { monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd, alertsEnabled, hardStopEnabled }, usage: { month, totalCostUsd, totalTokens, lastRunAt } } }
- PUT /cli/budgets (body: partial budgets) -> same payload as GET.
- Error (budget hard stop): status 402 or 403 with { ok:false, error:{ code:"budget_exceeded", message, caps:{ monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd }, usage:{ month, totalCostUsd }, projected:{ totalCostUsd, inputTokens, outputTokens, model } } }.
- Warning only (per-run over cap, hard stop disabled): include { warning:{ code:"budget_warning", message, capUsd, totalCostUsd } } in normal response.

Rollups:
- usage_rollups should be updated on actual billable runs only.
- Use UTC month boundaries; month stored as YYYY-MM string for deterministic queries.

Client expectations:
- CLI/web should treat missing /cli/budgets endpoint as "not available" and keep local-only UI/alerts.
- CLI can show budget status in `jfp status` only when endpoint returns data; otherwise fallback to config-only summary.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:00Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01: all open beads already in_progress; continuing bd-1ppu. Remaining work is premium-repo implementation (endpoints + persistence + enforcement). Blocked here until premium repo mirror/assignment. Suggested next steps: mirror bead in premium repo; implement /cli/budgets + /cli/budget-alerts; enforce hard-stop on run endpoints; add usage rollups + tests.","created_at":"2026-02-01T22:57:22Z"},{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01T23:23Z: top picks unchanged; all open beads already in_progress. Continuing bd-1ppu.

Concrete next steps (premium repo):
1) Add budgets table + budget_alerts + usage_rollups (UTC month key).
2) Implement GET/PUT /cli/budgets (auth + validate, return usage).
3) Implement GET /cli/budget-alerts (paging by before/limit).
4) Enforce hard stop in run/execute endpoints; return error code budget_exceeded with caps + usage + projected cost.
5) Add warning payload for per-run overages when hard stop disabled.
6) Tests: budget validation, rollups increment on actual usage, hard-stop block, warning path.

Public repo follow-up (after premium endpoints exist):
- Update CLI status to prefer /cli/budgets (fallback to local config).
- Wire web Budget Settings page to backend instead of localStorage; keep local-only fallback if endpoint missing.","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:24:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qkc","title":"core: export module tests (json.ts, yaml.ts, markdown.ts, skills.ts)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:27:51.347229657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:48:03.030241171Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T00:48:03.030207979Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"bd-1qkc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SCOPE: Test packages/core/src/export/ (json.ts, yaml.ts, markdown.ts, skills.ts, index.ts). APPROACH: Import real functions, pass real Prompt objects, verify output format/structure. Test: JSON export schema compliance, YAML valid parsing, markdown heading structure, SKILL.md frontmatter format. Verify round-trip: export → parse → verify fields match.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:30:39Z"},{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-1qkc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added json.test.ts (17 tests) and markdown.test.ts (24 tests) for JSON payload/prompt list and markdown generation. All mock-free.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:48:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qv8","title":"Security: Admin role must be server-derived","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:25:20.229414377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:41:07.985256805Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:41:07.985229053Z","close_reason":"Admin role now derived from JFP_ADMIN_ROLE env var instead of client-supplied x-jfp-admin-role header. Prevents privilege escalation. Updated layout.tsx, metadata page, and e2e tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
